{{infobox football tournament season
|title                      = Copa do Brasil
|year                       = 2021
|other_titles               =
|image                      = File:2021_Copa_do_Brasil_logo.png
|imagesize                  = 150 px
|caption                    = 
|country                    = Brazil
|dates                      = {{nowrap|9 March – 15 December 2021}}
|num_teams                  = 92
|champions                  = [[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]] {{small|(2nd title)}}
|runner-up                  = [[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]]
|continentalcup1            = [[2022 Copa Libertadores]]
|continentalcup1 qualifiers = [[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]
|matches                    = 122
|goals                      = 299
|top goal scorer            = [[Hulk (footballer)|Hulk]] (8 goals)
|player                     = [[Hulk (footballer)|Hulk]] (Atlético Mineiro)
|prevseason                 = [[2020 Copa do Brasil|2020]]
|nextseason                 = [[2022 Copa do Brasil|2022]]
}}

The '''2021 Copa do Brasil''' (officially the '''Copa Intelbras do Brasil 2021''' for sponsorship reasons)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/noticias/copa-brasil/cbf-lanca-copa-intelbras-do-brasil-2021 |title=CBF lança Copa Intelbras do Brasil 2021 |date=9 March 2021 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref> was the 33rd edition of the [[Copa do Brasil]] football competition. It was held between 9 March and 15 December 2021.

The competition was contested by 92 teams, either qualified by participating in their respective state championships (70), by the 2021 [[CBF ranking]] (10), by the [[2020 Copa do Nordeste]] (1), by the [[2020 Copa Verde]] (1), by the [[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série B|2020 Série B]] (1), by the [[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A|2020 Série A]] (1) or those qualified for [[2021 Copa Libertadores]] (8).

[[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]] defeated [[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]] 6–1 on aggregate in the finals to win their second title.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/competicoes/copa-brasil-masculino/2021/122 |title=Atlético-MG volta a vencer o Athletico-PR e é campeão da Copa Intelbras do Brasil |date=15 December 2021 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref> As champions, Atlético Mineiro qualified for the [[2022 Copa Libertadores]] group stage and the [[2022 Copa do Brasil]] third round. As Atlético Mineiro also won the [[2021 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]], they played in the [[2022 Supercopa do Brasil]] against the 2021 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A runners-up, [[Clube de Regatas do Flamengo|Flamengo]].<ref name="regulations">{{cite web|url=https://conteudo.cbf.com.br/cdn/202103/20210309221405_695.pdf |title=Regulamento Específico da Competição Copa do Brasil–2021 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>

[[Sociedade Esportiva Palmeiras|Palmeiras]] were the defending champions, but they were eliminated in the third round.

[[Hulk (footballer)|Hulk]] (Atlético Mineiro) and [[Everson (footballer, born 1990)|Everson]] (Atlético Mineiro) won best player and best goalkeeper awards, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ge.globo.com/futebol/times/atletico-mg/noticia/hulk-fecha-2021-com-mais-de-30-premios-individuais-e-encomenda-replicas-de-tacas-pelo-atletico-mg.ghtml |title=Hulk fecha 2021 com mais de 30 prêmios individuais e encomenda réplicas de taças pelo Atlético-MG |publisher=Globo |language=Portuguese |date=18 December 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/noticias/copa-brasil-masculino/luva-de-ouro-da-copa-do-everson-lembra-trajetoria-sempre-imaginei |title=Luva de Ouro da Copa do Brasil, Everson lembra trajetória no futebol: 'Orgulho da minha história' |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese |date=16 December 2021}}</ref>

==Format changes==
Starting from this edition, 92 teams contest the competition. Twelve teams advance directly to the third round instead of eleven advancing to the round of 16 as the previous editions. With this change there are only three initial rounds before the round of 16.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/noticias/copa-brasil/cbf-divulga-regulamento-da-copa-do-brasil-para-2021 |title=Com novo formato, Copa do Brasil 2021 terá uma fase a menos |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese |date=14 January 2021}}</ref>

==Qualified teams==
Teams in '''bold''' were qualified directly for the third round.
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!width=130|Association
!width=210|Team
!Qualification method
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Acre}} [[Federação de Futebol do Acre|Acre]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Galvez Esporte Clube|Galvez]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Acriano]] champions
|-
|[[Atlético Acreano]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Acriano]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Alagoas}} [[Federação Alagoana de Futebol|Alagoas]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Clube de Regatas Brasil|CRB]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Alagoano]] champions
|-
|[[Centro Sportivo Alagoano|CSA]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Alagoano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Murici Futebol Clube|Murici]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Alagoano#2021 Copa do Brasil play-off|2021 Copa do Brasil play-off]] winners
|-
|{{flagicon|Amapá}} [[Federação Amapaense de Futebol|Amapá]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Ypiranga Clube|Ypiranga]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Amapaense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Amazonas}} [[Federação Amazonense de Futebol|Amazonas]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Penarol Atlético Clube|Penarol]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Amazonense]] champions
|-
|[[Manaus Futebol Clube|Manaus]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Amazonense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Federação Bahiana de Futebol|Bahia]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Esporte Clube Bahia|Bahia]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Baiano]] champions
|-
|[[Alagoinhas Atlético Clube|Atlético de Alagoinhas]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Baiano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Sociedade Desportiva Juazeirense|Juazeirense]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Baiano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Vitória|Vitória]]
|4th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Federação Cearense de Futebol|Ceará]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Ceará Sporting Club|Ceará]]'''
|'''[[2020 Copa do Nordeste]] champions'''
|-
|[[Fortaleza Esporte Clube|Fortaleza]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Cearense]] champions
|-
|[[Guarany Sporting Club|Guarany de Sobral]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Cearense#First round|2020 Campeonato Cearense]] first round winners
|-
|[[Ferroviário Atlético Clube (CE)|Ferroviário]]
|2020 [[Copa Fares Lopes]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Espírito Santo}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Espírito Santo|Espírito Santo]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Rio Branco Futebol Clube|Rio Branco de Venda Nova]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Capixaba]] champions
|-
|[[Rio Branco Atlético Clube|Rio Branco]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Capixaba]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Distrito Federal}} [[Federação de Futebol do Distrito Federal|Federal District]]<br>{{small|2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Brasiliense F.C.|Brasiliense]]'''
|'''[[2020 Copa Verde]] champions'''
|-
|[[Sociedade Esportiva do Gama|Gama]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Brasiliense]] champions
|-
|[[Real Brasília FC|Real Brasília]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Brasiliense]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=5 |{{flagicon|Goiás}} [[Federação Goiana de Futebol|Goiás]]<br>{{small|3 + 2 berths}}
|[[Atlético Clube Goianiense|Atlético Goianiense]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Goiano]] champions
|-
|[[Goianésia Esporte Clube|Goianésia]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Goiano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Jaraguá Esporte Clube|Jaraguá]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Goiano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Goiás Esporte Clube|Goiás]]
|3rd best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Vila Nova Futebol Clube|Vila Nova]]
|10th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Maranhão}} [[Federação Maranhense de Futebol|Maranhão]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Sampaio Corrêa Futebol Clube|Sampaio Corrêa]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] champions
|-
|[[Moto Club de São Luís|Moto Club]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] runners-up 
|-
|[[Sociedade Esportiva Juventude|Juventude Samas]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Mato Grosso}} [[Federação Matogrossense de Futebol|Mato Grosso]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Nova Mutum Esporte Clube|Nova Mutum]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] champions
|-
|[[União Esporte Clube|União Rondonópolis]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Luverdense Esporte Clube|Luverdense]]{{ref label|id4|a|a}}
|2020 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Cuiabá Esporte Clube|Cuiabá]]
|7th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|{{flagicon|Mato Grosso do Sul}} [[Federação de Futebol de Mato Grosso do Sul|Mato Grosso do Sul]] {{small|1 berth}}
|[[Esporte Clube Águia Negra|Águia Negra]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Sul-Mato-Grossense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=6 |{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Federação Mineira de Futebol|Minas Gerais]]<br>{{small|4 + 1 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 3rd place'''
|-
|[[Tombense Futebol Clube|Tombense]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Mineiro]] runners-up
|-
|[[América Futebol Clube (MG)|América Mineiro]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Mineiro]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Caldense|Caldense]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Mineiro]] 4th place
|-
|[[Uberlândia Esporte Clube|Uberlândia]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Mineiro]] 5th place
|-
|[[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]]
|best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Pará}} [[Federação Paraense de Futebol|Pará]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Paysandu Sport Club|Paysandu]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paraense]] champions
|-
|[[Clube do Remo|Remo]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paraense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Castanhal Esporte Clube|Castanhal]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paraense]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Paraíba}} [[Federação Paraibana de Futebol|Paraíba]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Treze Futebol Clube|Treze]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paraibano]] champions
|-
|[[Campinense Clube|Campinense]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paraibano]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=6 |{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Federação Paranaense de Futebol|Paraná]]<br>{{small|4 + 1 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 9th place'''
|-
|[[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paranaense]] runners-up
|-
|[[FC Cascavel]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paranaense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Cianorte Futebol Clube|Cianorte]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paranaense]] 4th place
|-
|[[Operário Ferroviário Esporte Clube|Operário Ferroviário]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paranaense]] 5th place
|-
|[[Paraná Clube|Paraná]]
|8th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Pernambuco}} [[Federação Pernambucana de Futebol|Pernambuco]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Salgueiro Atlético Clube|Salgueiro]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Pernambucano]] champions
|-
|[[Santa Cruz Futebol Clube|Santa Cruz]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Pernambucano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Retrô Futebol Clube Brasil|Retrô]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Pernambucano#Standings|2020 Campeonato Pernambucano]] first round best placed team not already qualified
|-
|[[Sport Club do Recife|Sport]]
|2nd best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Piauí}} [[Federação de Futebol do Piauí|Piauí]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[4 de Julho Esporte Clube|4 de Julho]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Piauiense]] champions
|-
|[[Sociedade Esportiva Picos|Picos]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Piauiense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Rio de Janeiro|Rio de Janeiro]]<br>{{small|5 + 2 berths}}
|'''[[Clube de Regatas do Flamengo|Flamengo]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] champions'''
|-
|'''[[Fluminense FC|Fluminense]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 5th place'''
|-
|[[Botafogo de Futebol e Regatas|Botafogo]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Carioca]] 5th place
|-
|[[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Carioca]] 7th place
|-
|[[Volta Redonda FC|Volta Redonda]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Carioca#Torneio Extra|2020 Taça Independência]] winners
|-
|[[Boavista Sport Club|Boavista]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Carioca#Torneio Extra|2020 Torneio Extra]] winners
|-
|[[Madureira Esporte Clube|Madureira]]{{ref label|id4|b|b}}
|[[2020 Campeonato Carioca#Torneio Extra|2020 Torneio Extra]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{nowrap|{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Norte}} [[Federação Norte-Riograndense de Futebol|Rio Grande do Norte]]}} {{small|2 berths}}
|[[ABC Futebol Clube|ABC]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] champions
|-
|[[América Futebol Clube (RN)|América de Natal]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Sul}} [[Federação Gaúcha de Futebol|Rio Grande do Sul]]<br>{{small|4 + 2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] runners-up'''
|-
|'''[[Grêmio Foot-Ball Porto Alegrense|Grêmio]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 6th place'''
|-
|[[Sociedade Esportiva e Recreativa Caxias do Sul|Caxias]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Gaúcho]] runners-up
|-
|[[Clube Esportivo Bento Gonçalves|Esportivo]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Gaúcho]] 4th place
|-
|[[Ypiranga Futebol Clube|Ypiranga]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Gaúcho]] 5th place
|-
|[[Futebol Clube Santa Cruz|Santa Cruz]]
|2020 [[Copa FGF]] champions
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Juventude|Juventude]]
|6th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|{{flagicon|Rondônia}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado de Rondônia|Rondônia]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Porto Velho Esporte Clube|Porto Velho]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Rondoniense]] champions
|-
|{{flagicon|Roraima}} [[Federação Roraimense de Futebol|Roraima]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[São Raimundo Esporte Clube (RR)|São Raimundo]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Roraimense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=6 |{{flagicon|Santa Catarina}} [[Federação Catarinense de Futebol|Santa Catarina]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 + 2 berths}}
|'''[[Associação Chapecoense de Futebol|Chapecoense]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série B]] champions'''
|-
|[[Brusque Futebol Clube|Brusque]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Catarinense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Criciúma Esporte Clube|Criciúma]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Catarinense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Joinville Esporte Clube|Joinville]]
|2020 [[Copa Santa Catarina]] champions
|-
|[[Avaí FC|Avaí]]
|5th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Figueirense FC|Figueirense]]
|9th best placed team in the 2020 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=8 |{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Federação Paulista de Futebol|São Paulo]]<br>{{small|5 + 3 berths}}
|'''[[Sociedade Esportiva Palmeiras|Palmeiras]]'''
|'''[[2020 Copa Libertadores]] champions'''
|-
|'''[[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 4th place'''
|-
|'''[[Santos FC|Santos]]'''
|'''[[2020 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 8th place'''
|-
|[[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paulista]] runners-up
|-
|[[Mirassol Futebol Clube|Mirassol]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paulista]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Ponte Preta|Ponte Preta]]
|[[2020 Campeonato Paulista]] 4th place
|-
|[[Red Bull Bragantino]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Paulista#Campeonato Paulista do Interior|Campeonato Paulista do Interior]] winners
|-
|[[Marília Atlético Clube|Marília]]
|2020 [[Copa Paulista]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Sergipe}} [[Federação Sergipana de Futebol|Sergipe]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Associação Desportiva Confiança|Confiança]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Sergipano]] champions
|-
|[[Club Sportivo Sergipe|Sergipe]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Sergipano]] runners-up
|-
|{{flagicon|Tocantins}} [[Federação Tocantinense de Futebol|Tocantins]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Palmas Futebol e Regatas|Palmas]]
|2020 [[Campeonato Tocantinense]] champions
|}
:{{note label|id4||a}}{{small|As the 2020 [[Copa FMF]] was cancelled, its 2021 Copa do Brasil berth was awarded to the best placed team in the 2020 Campeonato Mato-Grossense not already qualified, [[Luverdense Esporte Clube|Luverdense]] (2020 Campeonato Mato-Grossense 3rd place).<ref>{{cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/mt/noticia/fmf-define-data-de-retorno-do-mato-grossense-e-cancela-disputa-da-copinha.ghtml |title=FMF define data de retorno do Mato-grossense e cancela disputa da Copinha |date=21 August 2020 |publisher=Globo |language=Portuguese}}</ref>}}
:{{note label|id4||b}}{{small|As the 2020 [[Copa Rio (state cup)|Copa Rio]] was cancelled, its 2021 Copa do Brasil berth was awarded to the Torneio Extra runners-up, [[Madureira Esporte Clube|Madureira]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://futeboldorio.com/?p=1624 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201113052805/http://futeboldorio.com/?p=1624 |url-status=usurped |archive-date=November 13, 2020 |title=FERJ confirma times na Série D e Copa do Brasil 2021 |date=12 November 2020 |publisher=Futebol do Rio |language=Portuguese}}</ref>}}

==Format==
The competition is a [[single-elimination tournament]], the first two rounds are played as a single match and the rest are played as a [[two-legged tie]]s. Twelve teams enter in the third round, which are teams qualified for 2021 Copa Libertadores (8), Série A best team not qualified for 2021 Copa Libertadores, Série B champions, Copa Verde champions and Copa do Nordeste champions. The remaining 80 teams play in the first round, the 40 winners play the second round, and the 20 winners play the third round. Finally, the sixteen third round winners advance to the round of 16.<ref name="regulations"/>

==Schedule==
The schedule of the competition is as follows:<ref>{{cite web|url=https://conteudo.cbf.com.br/cdn/202102/20210205191531_333.pdf |title=CALENDÁRIO DO FUTEBOL BRASILEIRO 2021 |publisher=CBF|language=Portuguese}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable"
|-
!Stage
!First leg
!Second leg
|-
|First round
|colspan=3|{{unbulleted list
|Week 1: 10 March
|Week 2: 17 March
|Week 3: 24 March
}}
|-
|Second round
|colspan=4|{{unbulleted list
|Week 1: 26 March
|Week 2: 1 April
|Week 3: 7 April 
|Week 4: 14 April
}}
|-
|Third round
|Week 1: 2 June<br>Week 2: 9 June
|Week 1: 9 June<br>Week 2: 16 June
|-
|Round of 16
|28 July
|Week 1: 31 July<br>Week 2: 4 August
|-
|Quarter-finals
|25 August
|15 September
|-
|Semi-finals
|20 October
|27 October
|-
|Finals
|12 December
|15 December
|}

==Draw==
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil first round|Draw}}
{{clear}}

==First round==
{{main|2021 Copa do Brasil first round}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil first round|Format}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil first round|First round}}

==Second round==
{{main|2021 Copa do Brasil second round}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil second round|Format}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil second round|Second round}}

==Third round==
{{main|2021 Copa do Brasil third round}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil third round|Draw}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil third round|Format}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil third round|Third round}}

==Final rounds==
{{main|2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Format}}

===Bracket===
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Bracket}}

===Round of 16===
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Draw3}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Round of 16}}

===Quarter-finals===
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Draw4}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Quarter-finals}}

===Semi-finals===
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Draw5}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Semi-finals}}

===Finals===
{{main|2021 Copa do Brasil Finals}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Draw6}}
{{#lst:2021 Copa do Brasil final rounds|Matches}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center; margin: 0 auto;"
|-
!2021 Copa do Brasil winners
|-
|'''[[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]'''<br>'''2nd Title'''
|}

==Top goalscorers==
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%"
!Rank
!Player
!Team
!{{Tooltip|1R|First round}}
!{{Tooltip|2R|Second round}}
!{{Tooltip|3R1|Third round 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|3R2|Third round 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|⅛F1|Round of 16 ("Eighth-finals") 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|⅛F2|Round of 16 ("Eighth-finals") 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|QF1|Quarter-finals 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|QF2|Quarter-finals 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|SF1|Semi-finals 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|SF2|Semi-finals 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|F1|Final 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|F2|Final 2nd leg}}
!Total
|-
!1
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Hulk (footballer)|Hulk]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=2| ||0||1||1||0||1||1||1||1||1||1
!8
|-
!rowspan=2|2
|align=left|{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Emiliano Rigoni]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=2| ||x||1||1||1||2||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=4 |
!rowspan=2|5
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Rossi (footballer)|Rossi]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Esporte Clube Bahia|Bahia]]
|3||1||0||0||0||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|-
!rowspan=5|4
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[David Corrêa da Fonseca|David]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Fortaleza Esporte Clube|Fortaleza]]
|1||0||0||2||0||0||0||1||x||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=2 |
!rowspan=5|4
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Pablo (footballer, born 1992)|Pablo]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=2| ||x||3||1||0||0||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=4 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Renato Kayzer]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=2| ||1||0||0||1||1||0||1||0||0||0
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Vanílson de Lima Silva|Vanílson]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Amazonas}} [[Manaus Futebol Clube|Manaus]]
|3||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Wellington Paulista]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Fortaleza Esporte Clube|Fortaleza]]
|0||0||1||0||2||1||0||0||x||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=2 |
|-
!rowspan=7|9
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Alef Manga]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Volta Redonda FC|Volta Redonda]]
|2||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
!rowspan=7|3
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Juninho Capixaba]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Esporte Clube Bahia|Bahia]]
|2||0||x||x||0||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Léo Gamalho]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]]
|1||2||0||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=8 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Michel Douglas Guedes|Michel Douglas]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Boavista Sport Club|Boavista]]
|1||1||0||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=8 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Pedro (footballer, born 1997)|Pedro]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Clube de Regatas do Flamengo|Flamengo]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=2| ||x||x||0||0||x||2||1||x||bgcolor="silver" colspan=2 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|CHI}} [[Eduardo Vargas]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=2| ||x||x||x||1||0||0||0||0||2||0
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Zé Roberto (footballer, born 1993)|Zé Roberto]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Goiás}} [[Atlético Clube Goianiense|Atlético Goianiense]]
|1||0||0||0||1||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|}
{{small|Source:[https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/competicoes/copa-brasil-masculino CBF]}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{Copa do Brasil}}
{{2021 in Brazilian football}}
{{2021 in South American football (CONMEBOL)}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Copa do Brasil 2021}}
[[Category:2021 Copa do Brasil| ]]
[[Category:Copa do Brasil seasons|2021]]
[[Category:2021 in Brazilian football]]
[[Category:2021 in domestic association football cups]]
